from pathlib import Path
import os

def sanitize_path(base_dir: str, user_path: str) -> str | None:
    # compute absolute base path
    base_path = Path(base_dir).resolve(strict=False)
    # join but if user_path is absolute, Path(user_path) will ignore base_path
    # Actually we want to treat user_path always relative? But we can't enforce; we just join.
    # So we can do candidate_path = base_path / Path(user_path)
    candidate = base_path / Path(user_path)
    # Resolve candidate path
    resolved = candidate.resolve(strict=False)
    # Ensure resolved path is within base_path
    try:
        # Use os.path.commonpath
        common = os.path.commonpath([str(base_path), str(resolved)])
        if common != str(base_path):
            return None
        # Ensure resolved path is absolute
        if not resolved.is_absolute():
            return None
        # Possibly canonicalize to string
        return str(resolved)
    except Exception:
        return None

But we need to consider if candidate.path is relative and the base_path is absolute; Path(base_dir).resolve(strict=False) ensures base_path is absolute. candidate = base_path / Path(user_path) will produce absolute path if user_path is relative; if absolute, will become absolute ignoring base_path. Good.

def sanitize_path(base_dir: str, user_path: str) -> str | None:
    # compute absolute base path
    base_path = Path(base_dir).resolve(strict=False)
    # join but if user_path is absolute, Path(user_path) will ignore base_path
    # Actually we want to treat user_path always relative? But we can't enforce; we just join.
    # So we can do candidate_path = base_path / Path(user_path)
    candidate = base_path / Path(user_path)
    # Resolve candidate path
    resolved = candidate.resolve(strict=False)
    # Ensure resolved path is within base_path
    try:
        # Use os.path.commonpath
        common = os.path.commonpath([str(base_path), str(resolved)])
        if common != str(base_path):
            return None
        # Ensure resolved path is absolute
        if not resolved.is_absolute():
            return None
        # Possibly canonicalize to string
        return str(resolved)
    except Exception:
        return None
